chapter_title.en: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types

text: 2715 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدٍ اَلْحَلَبِيِّ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ اَلْمُحْرِمُ يَنْظُرُ إِلَى اِمْرَأَتِهِ وَ هِيَ مُحْرِمَةٌ قَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2715 - It is narrated from Muhammad al-Halabi, who said: I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man in the state of ihram looking at his wife while she is also in the state of ihram.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it."
